strict=None changes only zip mismatch checking; fast EMA and slow EMA series are aligned to the same length before zipping.
Omitting strict keeps Python zip truncation behavior, but the two MACD series have matched lengths by construction.
strict=False cannot change emitted pairs because the fast series is sliced to slow_series length first.

For valid n>=10, the last Gaussian weight is 1.0 and earlier weights are positive, so wsum is greater than 1.0; both zero and one guards are unreachable.
strict=None is shape-only here because weights and prices are both length n from the same selected window.
Dropping the strict keyword does not alter the weighted sum because every generated weight has exactly one price.
strict=False only disables mismatch checking; no mismatch can occur after prices=closes[-n:] and range(n) weights.
